info.name: Anchor API
info.description:
Complete request collection for the Anchor e-commerce API (Laravel 12, Sanctum SPA cookie auth). Folders mirror docs/api-design.md's domains in user-journey order: Auth -> Catalog -> Cart/Checkout -> Orders/Payments/Returns -> Admin domains -> Reports/Settings/Audit -> Health.

## Auth setup (do this once per Postman session)
1. Enable this collection's cookie jar: Settings > General > 'Automatically follow redirects' and make sure Postman's cookie manager is allowed to store cookies for {{baseUrl}}.
2. Run 'Auth > Get CSRF Cookie' first. It hits Sanctum's /sanctum/csrf-cookie route and sets the XSRF-TOKEN cookie.
3. Run 'Auth > Login' (or Register). Cookies (session + XSRF-TOKEN) are stored automatically by Postman against {{baseUrl}}.
4. Every subsequent request automatically sends the session cookie. This collection's pre-request script also copies the XSRF-TOKEN cookie into an X-XSRF-TOKEN header on every request, matching what api-client.ts does in the real frontend — you don't need to do this by hand.
5. Admin endpoints require the logged-in user to hold the relevant Spatie permission (see each folder's description) — log in as a seeded Administrator, or promote a user via `php artisan tinker`.

## Known contract inconsistency (flagged, not fixed by this collection)
Customer-facing endpoints and Settings use camelCase body fields (`productId`, `shippingAddressId`, `emailOnNewOrder`). Admin write endpoints (categories/brands/products/warehouses/leads/quotations/coupons/banners/users/roles/inventory/order-status) currently use snake_case (`category_id`, `is_active`, `full_name`). The example bodies below match the API AS IT CURRENTLY BEHAVES so every request in this collection actually works — this is a pre-existing inconsistency worth fixing in the backend, not something to paper over here.

## Variables
`baseUrl` is the app root (no /api). Requests build `{{baseUrl}}/api/v1/...` themselves. ID variables (e.g. `{{categoryId}}`) are auto-populated by test scripts on the matching 'Create'/'Register' requests where practical — run folders top-to-bottom the first time through.

exec: // Mirrors what axios/api-client.ts does automatically for Sanctum SPA auth:
// read the XSRF-TOKEN cookie set by /sanctum/csrf-cookie and echo it back
// as a request header, since Postman's cookie jar sends the cookie but
// does not do this translation for you.

variable.description: App root, no /api suffix. Swap to your Herd domain (e.g. http://anchor-api.test) if not using `php artisan serve`.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Registration, login, and password recovery. First four endpoints are unauthenticated and rate-limited (throttle:auth, 5/min/IP).

request.description: Run this before Register/Login in a fresh Postman session so the XSRF-TOKEN cookie exists.
Creates the account and logs it in (session cookie set). Queues an email-verification job.
Use a seeded Administrator (see RolesAndPermissionsSeeder) before running the Admin folders.
Token comes from the password-reset email/notification log in local dev.
Requires an authenticated session (auth:sanctum).

item.name: Account
item.description: The logged-in customer's own shipping addresses. All require auth:sanctum.

request.description: Adds if absent, removes if present.
Max 4 products; the 5th toggle attempt is rejected.

item.name: Cart
item.description: Session-keyed for guests, user-keyed once logged in (CartResolver merges on login).

request.description: Preview-only; the same ValidateCouponAction runs for real inside Checkout.
throttle:checkout (10/min/user). Re-validates price and stock server-side regardless of the cart snapshot.
Only legal from `pending`/`processing` per the order state machine; restocks inventory.
Only allowed once the order is `delivered`; orderItemId comes from 'Get Order' response's items array.
orderId is the order's ULID (not the order_number). Only valid for an order awaiting mpesa payment.

Real Daraja calls are IP-allowlisted (mpesa.verify-ip middleware) and throttled (throttle:webhooks, 60/min). From Postman on localhost this will 403 unless your IP/allowlist config permits it — relax `services.mpesa.allowed_callback_ips` locally to test. CheckoutRequestID must match a payment already created by STK Push (idempotent on provider_reference).
role must be an existing Spatie role name: Administrator | Catalog Manager | Sales & Support | Marketing.
permissions.* must be existing Spatie permission names (products.manage, orders.manage, customers.manage, marketing.manage, reports.view, settings.manage, users.manage).

item.name: Admin: Catalog
item.description: Requires the `products.manage` permission. NOTE: request bodies here are snake_case — see the collection description.

body.raw:
{
  "category_id": "{{categoryId}}",
  "brand_id": "{{brandId}}",
  "name": "Dell XPS 13",
  "slug": "dell-xps-13",
  "sku": "DELL-XPS13-001",
  "short_description": "13-inch ultrabook.",
  "description": "Full product description here.",
  "price": 145000,
  "compare_at_price": 160000,
  "status": "active",
  "is_featured": true,
  "is_flash_deal": false,
  "meta_title": "Dell XPS 13",
  "meta_description": "Buy the Dell XPS 13 in Kenya.",
  "specifications": [
    { "group": "Performance", "label": "CPU", "value": "Intel Core i7" }
  ]
}

request.description: multipart/form-data. image: jpg/jpeg/png/webp, max 5MB.
Requires marketing.manage.
Backs both the Kanban and table admin views (api-design.md).
Enforced state machine: pending->processing->packing->shipped->delivered, with cancelled/refunded terminal branches. Illegal transitions return 422.
Approval restocks inventory and writes a refund payment record.
quantity is the new absolute quantity, not a delta; writes an audit trail via AdjustInventoryAction.
image_path is a pre-uploaded asset path/URL, not a multipart file upload.

item.name: Admin: Reports, AI Insights, Audit Logs & Settings
item.description: Reports/AI Insights/Audit Logs require `reports.view`; Settings requires `settings.manage`.

request.description: Cached 2 minutes; invalidated on new order/new customer.
Rows written nightly by `php artisan app:generate-ai-insights` — this endpoint only reads.
camelCase — unlike the rest of the Admin folder, Settings was built to match the customer-facing contract convention.
200 with {status:'ok', checks:{database,redis,queue}} when all reachable, else 503.
Laravel's built-in health route — only proves the app booted.
